query: I need to research three completely independent things before we start: the Kafka consumer group rebalancing behavior, our internal retry policy conventions, and how the new auth service handles token expiry. These can all be investigated in parallel.

We're evaluating five different vector DB options (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector) for our RAG pipeline and I need a structured research plan with orthogonality checks — don't want the tracks bleeding into each other

need to run parallel research on: (1) how competitors handle offline sync, (2) CRDT implementations in open source, (3) IndexedDB perf limits, (4) conflict resolution UX patterns. structured multi-agent approach pls

Before we architect the new observability layer I want independent tracks investigating: OTel collector bottlenecks, our current Grafana dashboards' query patterns, Prometheus cardinality issues, and what Datadog's agent actually costs us

spin up a proper multi-track research protocol for investigating why our p99 latency spiked — I want DB query analysis, network hop tracing, GC pause correlation, and cache eviction patterns all investigated independently then composed

research protocol for a 4-track investigation: GDPR data residency requirements, our current PII flows, encryption-at-rest options, and what the legal team flagged in the Q1 audit. need gates between phases
structured research across 6 independent failure hypotheses for the prod incident — want numbered plans and journal templates so the team can run them in parallel without stepping on each other

do a research-protocol style investigation: track 1 is webpack bundle analysis, track 2 is React rendering profiling, track 3 is network waterfall, track 4 is font/asset loading. compose findings at the end
I've got 3+ completely orthogonal research threads for the migration: legacy schema archaeology, target Postgres 16 feature set, and ETL tooling landscape. want prerequisite gates and formal dispatch

multi-track research needed — independently investigate our gRPC timeout config, the upstream SLA contracts, the circuit breaker thresholds, and historical timeout rates in Datadog. need structured composition at the end
research how postgres handles MVCC and vacuum so I know when to tune autovacuum for our write-heavy tables
I want to understand two things: how our current auth middleware works and what JWT rotation looks like in production. run hybrid-research on both
onboard me to the payments service — I'm new to this part of the codebase and need to understand how it fits together
extract patterns from these three OSS repositories for how they handle optimistic updates in offline-first apps
research the best approach and then implement the rate limiter for our API gateway
investigate these two competing approaches for our caching layer: Redis with write-through vs Memcached with write-behind. which fits our read/write ratio?
try to break our /api/v2/checkout endpoint — stress test it and find vulnerabilities before the launch
I want to deeply understand the architecture of this codebase before touching anything — map the subsystems, data flows, and runtime behavior
research what features competitors have that we don't before we start brainstorming the Q3 roadmap
look up the openai embeddings API docs and find the right parameters for our use case
